title: Dead Sea Scrolls Now for the internet
display_title: Dead Sea Scrolls Now for the internet
wikitext:
[]: Dead Sea Scrolls: Now for the internet Post by Stryker » Mon Sep 26, 2011 7:05 pm Read and Discuss. This could possibly open up some more information about Evangelion.. or it could just add more speculative bullshit. You Decide!

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Sep 26, 2011 7:49 pm We need the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls, it's improbable that there's anything useful in those scanned scrolls.

[]: Post by KnightmareX13 » Mon Sep 26, 2011 7:59 pm Those are probably the ones seele released because they contain no relevant info on the angels and are not considered a security risk.

[]: Post by CJD » Tue Sep 27, 2011 11:09 am KnightmareX13 wrote: Those are probably the ones seele released because they contain no relevant info on the angels and are not considered a security risk.

[]: Re: Dead Sea Scrolls: Now for the internet Post by Tankred » Tue Sep 27, 2011 11:14 am Stryker wrote: Read and Discuss. This could possibly open up some more information about Evangelion.. or it could just add more speculative bullshit. You Decide!

[]: Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Tue Sep 27, 2011 11:32 am CJD wrote: I do like that top comment though. For those of you who miss it, if you get the joke it's golden. Up next, Bethesda files lawsuit for use of the word "scrolls".

[]: Post by CJD » Tue Sep 27, 2011 11:41 am Fireand'chutes77 wrote: I don't get it. ( Are they talking about the Bethesda Naval Hospital?

[]: Post by driftking18594 » Tue Sep 27, 2011 11:47 am What sort of Dummkopf would think that SEELE would actually give such valuable information away?

[]: Post by John » Tue Sep 27, 2011 1:00 pm CJD wrote: Recently, they filed a lawsuit against "Notch", the creator of Minecraft, claiming his upcoming game, titled simply "Scrolls", infringes on their "The Elder Scrolls" trademark. The best part is, "Scrolls" is going to be a card battle game, similar to something like Magic or Yugioh, and "The Elder Scrolls" is a triple A blockbuster with top of the line graphics, an open world, and a heavy RPG system.

[]: Post by Aiko Heiwa » Tue Sep 27, 2011 3:11 pm John wrote: The most remarkable result was THIS Holy cow, the Dead Sea Scrolls were scanned with 1200 megapixels per image... Maybe the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls are in miniature between lines

[]: Post by John » Tue Sep 27, 2011 6:29 pm Only five of the total eight books have been digitized yet. Still waiting for the Digital Dead Sea Scrolls 2.22: You Can [Not] Touch Them Without Disintegrating.

[]: Post by Stryker » Tue Sep 27, 2011 6:31 pm Seele people are old bags who are too cheap to higher security. You do the math. Also, read.

[]: Post by Deepak » Tue Sep 27, 2011 7:36 pm these scrolls are talking a lot about jesus. I shall assume jesus is Shinji.

[]: Post by CJD » Tue Sep 27, 2011 7:43 pm Deepak wrote: these scrolls are talking a lot about jesus. I shall assume jesus is Shinji.

[]: Post by Final Messenger » Tue Sep 27, 2011 7:46 pm you know that the dead sea scrolls are a real thing in real life.

[]: Post by John » Tue Sep 27, 2011 7:51 pm But how can you define exactly "real life"? Jesus is Kaworu

[]: Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Tue Sep 27, 2011 8:15 pm Shinji was crucified jesus was crucified makes sense . also no one tell the church of nerv about the scrolls.

[]: Post by symbv » Tue Sep 27, 2011 9:01 pm bobbyfischer's ghost wrote: @symbv looks more like a saint to me.

[]: Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Sep 27, 2011 9:06 pm Deepak wrote: these scrolls are talking a lot about jesus. I shall assume jesus is Shinji.

[]: Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Tue Sep 27, 2011 9:11 pm symbv wrote: It won't be saints. If not the family of Jesus, it would be at least the Holy Trinity, which is not the same as saints or angels. As reference, this is Rublev's Holy Trinity icon. I think the Shinji pic takes inspiration from it: http://3.bp.blogspot.com/-W7PWpDfvvCg/TgibVbG8z1I/AAAAAAAAAKs/1XR96pyT2Gs/s1600/OL_Trinity.jpg
